facts.claim: A First Bus adult single in Bristol is £2.60 from 1 January 2026.
facts.prompt_hint: Use the exact £2.60 fare only when a fare comparison genuinely fits the live bus event.

facts.claim: First Bus adjusted operating profit rose 7% to £102.8 million in FY2026.
facts.prompt_hint: Contrast the £102.8 million figure with the observed service, without implying that one individual bus proves a company-wide trend.

facts.claim: FirstGroup reported an 8.8% adjusted operating margin for Regional Bus in FY2026, down from 9.7% in FY2025.
facts.prompt_hint: The direction matters: profit rose, but the Regional Bus margin fell from 9.7% to 8.8%. Do not say the margin rose.

facts.claim: FirstGroup recorded 390.4 million Regional Bus passenger journeys in FY2026, down from 403.3 million in FY2025.
facts.prompt_hint: Use the exact annual totals. Do not present this national Regional Bus figure as Bristol-only data.

facts.claim: FirstGroup returned £89 million to shareholders through dividends and its FY2026 £50 million buyback, then announced a further £100 million buyback.
facts.prompt_hint: Keep the completed £89 million return separate from the newly announced £100 million programme.

facts.claim: FirstGroup adjusted revenue rose 25% to £1.716 billion in FY2026.
facts.prompt_hint: This is a FirstGroup-wide figure, not Bristol or First Bus revenue.

facts.claim: FirstGroup chief executive Graham Sutherland received £2.974 million total remuneration in FY2026, including £2.337 million of annual bonus and long-term incentive awards.
facts.prompt_hint: Say total remuneration, not salary. His salary was £605,700; £2.337 million came from the two variable-pay columns.

facts.claim: FirstGroup said Regional Bus lost mileage fell from 1.8% to 1.5% in FY2026 and its Net Promoter Score rose from 11 to 17.
facts.prompt_hint: This is a positive operational result. Give credit where it is due and do not claim it describes today's one observed journey.

facts.claim: FirstGroup reported about £190 million of accelerated capital expenditure, principally on First Bus electrification, with 26% of its UK bus fleet zero-emission by March 2026.
facts.prompt_hint: This is another positive result. Do not imply all Bristol buses are zero-emission.

occasions.label: Bristol Bus Boycott anniversary
occasions.prompt_hint: On 30 April 1963, the Bristol Bus Boycott began its challenge to the Bristol Omnibus Company's colour bar. If used, be respectful and factual rather than jokey.

source.title: Bristol's day marking the 60th anniversary of the city's iconic bus boycott

occasions.label: World Bicycle Day
occasions.prompt_hint: The United Nations observes World Bicycle Day on 3 June. A brief friendly nod to another form of sustainable transport is enough.

occasions.label: Windrush Day
occasions.prompt_hint: National Windrush Day is 22 June. If used, recognise the Windrush generation's contribution to Britain, including public transport, without turning it into a punchline.

source.title: Annual day of celebrations for the Windrush Generation

occasions.label: London buses went cashless
occasions.prompt_hint: London's bus network became cash-free on 6 July 2014. Keep any comparison practical and do not imply Bristol no longer accepts cash.

source.title: TfL introduces Oyster One More Journey on London's buses

occasions.label: Catch the Bus Month
occasions.prompt_hint: Bus Users UK runs Catch the Bus Month throughout September 2026. Mention it at most once in a day and only when it naturally fits the observed service.

occasions.label: World Car Free Day
occasions.prompt_hint: World Car Free Day is observed on 22 September. Keep the reference about giving alternatives a try, not scolding people who need cars.

occasions.label: UK bus deregulation anniversary
occasions.prompt_hint: The Transport Act 1985 bus deregulation provisions took effect on 26 October 1986 outside London. Keep the distinction between the 1985 Act and its 1986 commencement.

occasions.label: World Sustainable Transport Day
occasions.prompt_hint: The United Nations observes World Sustainable Transport Day on 26 November. A restrained mention of buses as public infrastructure is enough.

news.label: England £2 bus fare cap announced
news.claim: On 22 July 2026, the government announced that participating bus services in England outside London will have a £2 cap on single fares from 1 January 2027 until the end of 2027.
news.prompt_hint: Use the exact dates. Do not say the cap applies now, and do not say today or yesterday. Bristol's current First Bus adult single is £2.60, from the separately sourced fare fact.

source.title: Cheaper travel for millions with a third off fares
